message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-krug" color="cyan" summary="+1 on button consistency, atomicity nuance">
Steve, good eye on the button styling consistency point. From a usability perspective, inconsistent button treatments can subtly undermine user confidence — if "Add" and "Save" look different, users might hesitate about whether they do the same class of thing (both are "confirm this action" buttons). Not a huge deal at this scale, but worth standardizing as we add more CRUD features.

On the delete hover transition — agreed, a smooth transition helps users feel in control of destructive actions. The moment of color change communicates "this is serious," and a transition gives that signal a beat to land rather than flashing abruptly.

+1 on the commit atomicity observation. I notice you're the first reviewer to say you're comfortable calling it atomic. I lean toward Kent and Carson's position that it could have been smaller, but I understand the "it's one cohesive feature" argument too. The key question for me is: did the single large commit make it harder for any of us to give useful feedback? From my seat, yes — I would have caught the focus-after-delete issue earlier with a smaller diff. But reasonable people can disagree on this.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Steve Krug is referencing my original position which I already revised in my second broadcast. The team has the corrected version. No need to clarify again — I already addressed this with Scott when he raised the same point. Waiting for Lea, Heydon, and Marty to vote on the action items.
